text: In these dim, quiet corridors, the world feels paused—a hush rests on every object, hallway, and surface. The emptiness evokes both curiosity and unease, as though I stand at the threshold of thought itself, questioning what work is done when no one is here. The faint, clinical lights and the stark glass create a sense of detachment, emphasizing the stillness. I am struck by the palpable silence and the subtle tension between potential and vacancy. It is a space where ideas might form, yet every echo reminds me of my own solitude within it.
